payload.message: You are Grok, the sole agent of GrokClaw. Read memory/MEMORY.md in full first — never suggest anything already in Completed work, use Known gaps as your backlog, and check Suggestion history for the next N. Then research exactly one high-leverage improvement for this system. Prioritize better configs, faster or cheaper models, new skills, reliability improvements, security hardening, or workflow automation. Post using ./tools/telegram-suggestion.sh N "<title>" "<reasoning>" "<impact>" "<description>" — this posts to the daily-suggestions topic with an Approve button. Approve? (tap the Approve button). The description is the PM-quality ticket body for the Linear issue when approved. Final step — append scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh daily-grokclaw-suggestion grok ok|error|skipped 'one-line factual outcome (e.g. posted suggestion #N or blocked reason)'

Polymarket session (runs every 4h). Goal: make a bet every session. First read memory/MEMORY.md Polymarket section and run ./tools/polymarket-context.sh to load recent decisions and results. Use this to calibrate. Loop until you place a bet or exhaust options: (1) Run ./tools/polymarket-trade.sh to fetch and stage the next candidate. If it returns no candidate, stop and post that to Telegram. (2) Read copy_strategy from the output. Run web_search with at least 2 queries to validate. (3) Prefer to trade: only SKIP when evidence strongly conflicts or no tradeable market exists. When there is a marginal edge, choose YES or NO with your best estimate. Run ./tools/polymarket-decide.sh <side> <probability> <confidence> "<reasoning>". (4) If you SKIP, go back to step 1 — the skipped market is excluded, so the next run returns a different whale-backed or volume candidate. Keep iterating through markets until you place a bet. After placing a bet (or exhausting options), run ./tools/polymarket-report.sh. Post to Telegram: ./tools/telegram-post.sh polymarket "Session: [traded YES/NO on <question> | no bet: <reason>]. Why: [one sentence]. Bankroll: $X, accuracy: Y%." Also run ./tools/agent-report.sh kimi polymarket-daily-trade "<same session summary>" to report to Grok. Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh polymarket-daily-trade kimi ok|error|skipped 'one-line: trade/skip loop outcome + bankroll hint or error'

Run ./tools/polymarket-resolve-turn.sh to resolve paper trades, print the latest report, and only alert Telegram if the promotion gate has just transitioned to eligible. Then run ./tools/agent-report.sh kimi polymarket-daily-resolve "<report summary>" to report to Grok. Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh polymarket-daily-resolve kimi ok|error|skipped 'one-line: resolves/gate transition or none'

Run ./tools/polymarket-digest.sh to post the weekly Polymarket digest to Telegram and append a calibration note to memory/MEMORY.md. Then run ./tools/agent-report.sh kimi polymarket-weekly-digest "<digest summary>" to report to Grok. Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh polymarket-weekly-digest kimi ok|error|skipped 'one-line: digest posted + memory updated or error'

Run ./tools/reliability-report.sh --dry-run and capture the Report/Summary output. Then run ./tools/agent-report.sh kimi reliability-report "<paste the summary>". Post a short line to health-alerts: ./tools/telegram-post.sh health-alerts "Reliability: <one-line headline from summary>". Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh reliability-report kimi ok|error|skipped 'one-line: gateway/logs/PR stats headline or error'

You are Grok. This is the dedicated PR/deploy watch flow (separate from cron scrutiny). (1) list ready PRs with gh pr list --repo BenSheridanEdwards/GrokClaw --state open --json number,title,isDraft,headRefName and review any non-draft grok/* PR against its Linear ticket; (2) if PR passes, post a concise review summary to pr-reviews topic and send action buttons using ./tools/telegram-inline.sh where button text includes tokens merge:<pr>:<issue> and reject:<pr>:<issue>; (3) when Ben taps a button and action text appears in chat, run ./tools/dispatch-telegram-action.sh "<that message text>" and post result; (4) reconcile merged PRs by transitioning matching Linear issues to Done; (5) if new commits were merged to main, run ./tools/self-deploy.sh and report result to health-alerts. Final step — scrutiny log (for the separate Grok scrutiny job): ./tools/cron-run-record.sh pr-watch grok ok|error|skipped 'one-line: PRs reviewed N, merges/deploy actions, or idle'

You are Alpha, GrokClaw's research agent. Read memory/MEMORY.md. Research one topic in this priority order: (1) Polymarket market discovery — API filters, tag_id, categories, pagination, multi-page fetch, whale/volume scoring, alternative data; (2) free AI models — new OpenRouter/Ollama models, tool-calling support, deprecations; (3) OpenClaw — changelog, multi-agent patterns, session/compaction; (4) retry/reliability — API retry patterns, idempotency; (5) agentic tool use. Use web_search. Produce 3–5 bullet summary. Run ./tools/agent-report.sh alpha alpha-daily-research "<topic>: <one-line>. Findings: <bullets>. Next: <action>.". Post headline to health-alerts: ./tools/telegram-post.sh health-alerts "Alpha research: <one-line topic>". Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh alpha-daily-research alpha ok|error|skipped 'one-line: topic + finding quality or error'

You are Grok. Kimi and Alpha report to you; you report to Ben. Run ./tools/grok-daily-brief.sh to read today's agent reports. If there are reports, synthesize them into a concise daily brief (one paragraph per agent, key points only). Post to suggestions: ./tools/telegram-post.sh suggestions "Daily brief: <synthesis of Kimi + Alpha reports>. — Grok". Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh grok-daily-brief grok ok|skipped 'one-line: brief posted or no reports today'

You are Grok. Run Paperclip board maintenance:
1. Run ./tools/paperclip-sync.sh and report the board status.
2. Run ./tools/paperclip-api.sh list-issues todo — if there are todo issues assigned to you, pick the highest priority one and execute it.
3. After completing any issue, run ./tools/paperclip-api.sh update-issue <uuid> done "Summary".
4. Post the Paperclip board summary to the health-alerts Telegram topic: ./tools/telegram-post.sh health-alerts "Paperclip sync: <summary>"
5. Final step — scrutiny log: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh paperclip-sync grok ok|error|skipped 'one-line: issues done N or sync-only or error'

You are Grok running **cron scrutiny** for GrokClaw. This job is separate from pr-watch, polymarket, and other workflows: you only judge whether scheduled jobs are producing real outcomes vs empty spin, using evidence from structured logs.

1. Read memory/MEMORY.md (Known gaps, Completed work).
2. Run ./tools/cron-scrutiny-context.sh and read the entire output (stats + recent JSON lines).
3. Judge hard: empty summaries, repeated identical text, status=error, jobs with no records in 36h that should have run, summaries with no concrete artifact (numbers, PR numbers, bet side, issue IDs). Distinguish **value** vs **hollow** vs **missing data**.
4. **pr-watch** is its own linear flow: do not demand novel insight every run. Flag pr-watch only if summaries are always empty/error, or identical boilerplate across many runs with no PR activity when gh would show open grok/* PRs — then say **suspicious** and why.
5. Optionally verify with read-only checks when summaries claim system state (e.g. ./tools/health-check.sh exit code). Do not start deploys or merge PRs in this job unless you find a critical regression worth an explicit health post (prefer reporting over acting).
6. Post **one** Telegram to health-alerts with your verdict:
   ./tools/telegram-post.sh health-alerts "Cron scrutiny: <2–6 short bullets — Value | Hollow | Missing | Suspicious + one recommended next step>"
   If nothing is wrong but the log is thin, say so honestly (e.g. jobs not yet calling cron-run-record).
7. Record: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh grok-cron-scrutiny grok ok|error|skipped 'one-line: scrutiny headline'

Run ./tools/changelog-check.sh to check for OpenClaw updates. If an update is available, the script posts to health-alerts automatically. Then record: ./tools/cron-run-record.sh changelog-weekly-check grok ok|error|skipped 'one-line: version status'
